question.content: For a rope stretching uniformly from length L at rate v while an ant crawls at speed u relative to the rope, compute the time needed to reach the far end. Output exactly one expression token sequence in plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no unit text, no sentence, no equality sign, no chained equalities, and no extra text. Answer format: a single algebraic expression. Use the variable names specified in the problem and ASCII characters only; do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Discretize the rope into many equal material segments and analyze incremental uniform stretching.
step_1.key_point: Track ant progress by segment fractions, not by continuous-coordinate ODE setup.

step_3.content: At each small time step, every segment scales by the same factor while the ant advances u*dt relative to the local rope.
step_3.key_point: The cumulative advance becomes a harmonic-like sum over stretch factors.

step_5.content: Take the continuum limit of the discrete sum, which converges to integral(dt/(L+v*t)).
step_5.key_point: The integral appears as the N->infinity limit of the discrete process.

step_7.content: Set total advanced fraction to 1 for reaching end B and solve the exponential relation for reach time.
step_7.key_point: Endpoint condition closes the model and yields t_c.
